summary: Idempotency Protection Bug Fix Testing - All 13 features verified working. Backend idempotency protection prevents duplicate project creation. Profile page shows friendly stage labels, progress bars, and delete buttons. No duplicate rows in In Progress section.

details: POST /api/story-video-studio/projects/create with idempotency_key creates new project
Returns success=true, project_id, and message 'Project created successfully'
Second POST with SAME idempotency_key returns existing project_id
Message indicates 'duplicate request detected' or 'existing project'
No new project created - prevents duplicate rows
POST with DIFFERENT idempotency_key creates a NEW project
Different project_ids returned for different keys
POST without idempotency_key still creates project normally
Backward compatible with old clients
GET /api/story-video-studio/projects with auth token extracts user_id from JWT
Returns only the authenticated user's projects
All returned projects have matching user_id
GET /api/story-video-studio/projects without auth falls back to 'test_user'
Returns test_user's projects or empty list
GET /api/story-video-studio/projects returns only parent projects
All returned projects have parent_project_id=None
Child/continuation projects are filtered out
Duplicate idempotency_key records are collapsed
Only one project per idempotency_key returned
Most progressed version is kept
DELETE /api/story-video-studio/projects/{project_id} deletes project
Associated assets (scene_assets, voice_tracks, render_jobs) also deleted
Returns success=true with 'deleted' message
DELETE non-existent project returns 404
Proper error handling
Profile page 'In Progress' section shows friendly stage labels
Format: 'Step X/5: Stage Name'
Verified: 'Step 1/5: Draft Created' visible for draft projects
Each in-progress project has a progress bar
Progress bar shows percentage based on stage (20% for draft, 40% for scenes, etc.)
Amber/green color scheme
Each in-progress project has a delete button
data-testid='delete-project-{id}' attribute present
Trash icon visible
Profile page does NOT show duplicate rows for same-key projects
2 unique projects displayed with 2 unique project IDs
Idempotency collapse working correctly

backend_story_video_studio_py.create_project_idempotency_check: Lines 302-314 - Checks for existing project with same user_id + idempotency_key
backend_story_video_studio_py.list_projects_auth_extraction: Lines 446-456 - Extracts user_id from JWT token, falls back to test_user
backend_story_video_studio_py.list_projects_collapse_logic: Lines 466-494 - Collapses duplicates by idempotency_key, keeps most progressed

frontend_profile_js.stage_map: Lines 483-488 - Maps status to friendly labels (Step X/5: Stage Name)
frontend_profile_js.progress_bar: Lines 505-507 - Progress bar with percentage based on stage
frontend_profile_js.delete_button: Lines 519-529 - Delete button with data-testid and onClick handler

critical_code_review_comments: All idempotency protection features are correctly implemented
Backend correctly checks for existing project with same user_id + idempotency_key before creating new
list_projects correctly extracts user_id from JWT token instead of defaulting to test_user
list_projects correctly filters to parent-only projects and collapses duplicates
Frontend generates unique idempotency_key using crypto.randomUUID() with fallback
Profile page correctly displays friendly stage labels, progress bars, and delete buttons
No duplicate rows observed in Profile page In Progress section
seed_data_creation: Test projects created and cleaned up during pytest execution
context_for_next_testing_agent: Idempotency protection is fully working. The test user (test@visionary-suite.com) has 2 draft projects in 'In Progress' state. Backend tests create and cleanup test projects automatically. Profile page UI shows friendly stage labels like 'Step 1/5: Draft Created' with progress bars and delete buttons. MongoDB index user_idempotency_idx is in place for efficient lookups.
